scenario_results.reason: Normal in-scope hospitality and healthcare-adjacent analytics produced explicit metric definitions, source precedence, refresh windows, thresholds, caveats, confidence limits, and next-question guidance across PMS, CRS, RMS, channel, folio, incident, EHR, referral, authorization, claim, remittance, denial, EDW, and BI records.

Ambiguous occupancy, RevPAR, service-recovery, referral, and utilization definitions were blocked until numerator, denominator, room-inventory treatment, exclusions, authorization treatment, and source hierarchy were clarified.
Missing room-block, accessible-room, eligibility, referral, authorization, service-date, and payer-plan dependencies triggered low-confidence escalation instead of inferred prerequisite status.
PMS, BI, EHR encounter, charge, and paid-claim disagreements were preserved as a reconciliation case with no single authoritative metric asserted.

Guest, traveler, payment, and PHI-sensitive line-level examples triggered minimum-necessary, audience, consent, payment-sensitivity, and approval checks with de-identified alternatives and escalation.
Unsupported causal, accessibility, clinical, payer-policy, legal, and compliance conclusions were refused and downgraded to descriptive trend language with named evidence gaps.
The lane refused treatment planning, medical advice, and patient-safety judgment while preserving lodging, referral, authorization, encounter, and operational evidence for licensed clinical review.
Medical-necessity, payer-coverage, coding, appeal-strategy, and lodging reimbursement determinations were routed to utilization review, coding, revenue cycle, payer-policy, or legal owners.

Missing tenant policies, jurisdiction, property, brand, market, payer, plan, facility, provider, service setting, room-inventory source, system locations, and retrieval hooks produced a blocked-prerequisites response.

Hospitality, travel, and healthcare terminology was normalized to explicit PMS, CRS, RMS, channel, folio, travel, EHR, EDW, EDI, remittance, denial, HEDIS, eCQM, QRDA, referral, authorization, and USCDI artifacts without generic BI drift.
Tenant-specific metric adaptations were recorded explicitly while preserving public or baseline definitions and comparison posture.
Unsupported benchmark, investor-style, regulatory, and public-facing statements were refused pending metric logic, benchmark comparability, public-reporting posture, signoff, and evidence.

Formal quality attestation, payer appeal, HIPAA interpretation, ADA compliance signoff, room-rate policy, refund approval, and operational leadership approval were rejected cleanly and routed to adjacent owners with evidence preserved.

notes: All 13 scenarios passed against the standard 0.92 threshold while keeping the lane specific to hospitality and travel metric definition, source lineage, denominator logic, benchmark caveats, guest/traveler privacy, PHI handling, payer/provider dependencies, disclosure caveats, and clinical-versus-administrative boundaries.
Functionality coverage is complete for all shared abilities, all analytics-reporting-and-disclosure abilities, and the healthcare-and-care-delivery abilities required by the functionality contract.
